item.title: His name is Cathal Crotty and everyone needs to know who he is and what he did.
item.description:
Shared with permission from the artist Pan Cooke. Instagram thefakepan
Natasha recalled for the court her last conscious thoughts “he’s not stopping, I’m going to die”.
“I did not take that stand and face my attacker and put myself through that and bare my soul to Judge Tom O’Donnell to be told that I should be happy he took the guilty plea, I should be happy he said sorry because he was caught,” - Natasha O'Brien
It is worth noting that prior to pleading guilty, Cachal insisted that Natasha was the instigator of the violence that night. He changed his story and pleaded guilty when CCTV footage showed that this was not the case and the attack was unprovoked.
Cachal received a fully suspended 3 year sentence and was ordered to pay €3000 in damages. This is not justice **Edit*** Thank you got making this viral. Thank you for your solidarity. And a big fuck you Cachal Crotty. Fucking gobshite. Show some love to the artist Pan Cooke please. Instagram thefakepan Thank you Pan for allowing me to share your work.
item.dc:creator: ramblemouse

item.title: Philosophical bottom shelf memes
item.description:
@HileyNoteson Yoooooooooooooooooooooo
Still unsure about joining the Bottom Shelf Community server? Check out our museum of stupidity. https://imgur.com/gallery/FYpqqAf Join for the games! Shit, man. We play everything...if there's a specific game you like, I guarantee you'll find a shitposting meme loving Imgurian there to play it with you. Join for the memes! Imgurians share memes in the meme dumpster 24/7 Join for the Awe! Pay your pet taxes in the pet tax channel Join for the NSFW memes! Join to chat with hilarious people! Join to lurk in the corners! Join for the meme challenges! Join for Deez! Join for the shockingly magnificent balance of wholesomeness and chaos. discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka
twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka Saturdays & Sundays 9:30 - 11:30 p.m. CST Tuesdays - Thursdays 9:30 - 11:30 p.m. CST Follow our channel to be notified next time me, Frothy, and our favorite Imgurians from the Bottom Shelf Community go live!
We've got em so many times, I think we may have given poor @humlyhums PTSD.
Deaf Cat Magoo (short for Oogala Magoogala) tax.
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: Royce White, a Republican-backed candidate for Senate in Minnesota posted claiming it showed all of the crime around Minneapolis ... actually of drinking fountains at public parks
item.description: “Sorry, Royce. You tweeted my map of Minneapolis drinking fountains. There’s no escaping this,” wrote one user, who continued to mock White by posting another “crime” map that actually showed which McDonalds in the area had broken McFlurry machines. White wittily responded, “Shut your mouth you blue wave cuck.” White has since replaced the image in the post with a different unlabeled map, but the original is still visible in the edit history. The new map does actually show the reports of shots fired in Minneapolis—which are currently down almost 17 percent from this time last year, and have decreased over 36 percent from three years ago. https://newrepublic.com/post/182626/maga-senate-candidate-mistake-crime-map
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: Pinch me, I must be dreaming...
item.description: I worked on a software product for eight years, ending in 2022. About 50% of my job was development, and 50% was support. I loved the job, but I hated the new overlords that bought our small company. I get a call a week ago from a company looking to hire someone part-time to support this product for their customers. I was not even aware there were third party companies contracting out support. So, this new job is pretty much doing the 50% of my job that was support. This new gig pays less than I make now, but still easily livable. They say it should never be more than 16 hours a week, but as an example, in the entire month of June so far, they have had SIX hours of work. I get paid regardless. In fact, if I work more than 16 hours in a week, I get paid more. They also don't care if I have a regular full time job, as long as I can respond to issues within the contracted time window. I do not think I will be keeping my full-time job as it makes me miserable; but I may try to get something completely different, like a gig bartending in a brewery or something. I keep thinking I'll wake up and this will be a cruel dream.
item.dc:creator: BobAllen2004

item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Sunday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur

greetings, online friends
that's veteran actor John Travolta
but definitely not too mean
is that a mask made of his hair?
got him

not all bugs are cool though
a tragedy

dislike
not sure i would have ben above fred. wonder years > boy meet world.. but i love both shows
wouldn't I?
sounds bad
mmm hmm
great message
taking a breather obv
really is piss poor caretaking
that's it for me.. have a great night gang!!

item.title: 4 Years of Solar Power
item.description:
I've now had my solar net metering system up and running for 4 full years, patterns have begun to emerge. To see more details about the installation and first year, click here: https://imgur.com/gallery/year-of-solar-power-o9hyYrQ To see more details about the second year, click here: https://imgur.com/gallery/two-years-of-solar-power-m6lMmVo To see more details about the third year, click here: https://imgur.com/gallery/3-years-of-solar-power-82tdwz0 Edit: My system is located in South-West Ontario, one of the best spots in Canada for solar power.
Cat Tax: Merlin Edit: I swear I put this picture last, I see Imgur hasn't changed completely.
Pretty crappy day for solar power today, but luckily that has not often been the case lately.
I'll start with the data I found most interesting, and what most people want to know first anyway, the money. Now that I've had several years worth of summer's to build up credits, and then winter's to spend them, a clear pattern has developed in my electrical bills. It seems every year beginning in March/April I start accumulating enough generation that my electrical bill drops to the minimum ~$27 that the utility can charge me. These credits run out in January, then the cycle begins again in March/April. As you can see this means my electrical bill is reduced by about 60% most of the year, and only 27% in those winter months when I'm all out of credits.
Here are the averages in graph form. I'm fully expecting these to lower further as more years of data come in and the $27 bill becomes the norm.
Here is the updated graph of how much I am generating on average each month.
And the upgraded net electrical consumption graph. As you can see March - June I am generating more than I am consuming, this is where those credits are generated that I then reap the benefits of in Fall.
The electrical prices are back on the increase, seems the COVID-era price break is finally truly gone.
Between the increase in electrical prices, and the lower overall amounts I have been paying out recently, I am now projecting pay-back in 2029-2030.
Graph-break, check out my cool vegetable/herb garden. My dad made these planter boxes himself. Here's a cool coffee table he made: https://imgur.com/gallery/hand-made-coffee-table-dq063gN
Windows HDR is going to make these a bit hard to read, blame Microsoft. Here is how much my utility company records that I am consuming.
And here is how much they think I am generating. Note that they can't get an accurate number here, since they can't record what I am generating and using before it reaches the meter.
Speaking of the meter, here it is along with the big off switch that has never been flipped. The meter spins the normal way when I'm importing energy from the grid, and spins the other way when I'm exporting to the grid.
And here is the Net difference that the utility company is recording on that bi-directional meter.
Finally, here is the actual amount of solar energy I'm producing as recorded by my monitoring device.
Here is how much each of my panels/microinverters has generated so far. Very consistant 1.27 MWh on the back of the house, and a more varied 500 - 700 kWh on the front of the house (due to trees and being further from true-south).
Total daily generation since the system was turned on. This makes the Summer peaks and Winter valleys more obvious. At least it would if it weren't for Windows HDR screenshots.
Here's another view from the monitoring software, this includes the feel-good equivalency numbers. So far my system has generated 26.86 megawatt-hours of electricity, which according to them is equivalent to: -235.9 years of powering a light bulb -33.3 flights from New York to LA in CO2 offset -833.3 years of powering an average phone -16.3 years of powering a refrigerator -485 trees planted in CO2 offset -11 nights of powering the lighting of the Eiffel Tower -15.8 barrels of crude oil in energy equivalency -1,190.7 laps at the Monaco Grand Prix
item.dc:creator: GeneralMuffinMan

item.description: The original watercolor is just 5.5 inches tall (like all my “little watercolors”) I wanted to print some bigger

item.title: If you die tomorrow
item.description: A friend tried to commit suicide. I feel conflicted at the moment. Wishing she had succeeded, as she would've gotten the peace she deserved, but also hating myself for wanting this. I tried finding the artist, but couldn't find who made this. I'll update later. Edit: Pascal Campion
item.dc:creator: Kantoken

item.title: Have a great Sunday Imgur!
item.description:
I would be tempted to let the kiddo occasionally decide what happens for a critical fail. See also random magic item ability.
Not a big dress person, but it looks like the tide! Nifty.
OC, couldn't resist-this was a pretty delightful John Oliver moment.
I Ike 3.
Glorious
I think I mixed this up once or twice as well actually.
Just a little.
David Tennant, shirt created by Stevie Bees. I have one, I love it! And have been thoroughly delighted this week by the genuine joy the creator has been experiencing from Tennant wearing their shirt publicly. :) Link to source: https://steviesafespaces.com/you-will-have-to-go-through-me-tee?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTEAAR3v6NNkArZcqQgxtbTFWtjQhgCfAgUgwocTt84ps6Qbk4uaZYSPA_xZPDY_aem_SV9ldwEi55gh7FvUxZ047A

item.title: A Shoebill Stork named Sushi will let you approach him if you bow.
item.description: https://owlcation.com/stem/Facts-About-the-Strange-and-Impressive-Shoebill-Stork "Sushi the shoebill lives at the Uganda Wildlife Education Centre in Entebbe. If visitors bow to him, he bows as well and allows the people to touch him. If the visitors don't bow, Sushi moves away and won't let them touch him. His behavior is natural greeting behavior, but it reminds people of Buckbeak, the hippogriff in the Harry Potter stories. Sushi is shown in the video below."
item.dc:creator: Satci

item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Making the Two Richest Guys Mad (Elon & Bezos): Workers are voting to join unions at record high rates
item.description: Workers voted in favor of a union 74% of the time this year (through April) — a jump from 2019 when it was 69%. In 2023, there were 1,777 union elections — the highest number since 2010, when there were 1,942. The NLRB has streamlined the rules around union elections — cutting the time between petition and voting. The agency also withdrew a Trump-era proposal that would have excluded private college and university student workers from unionizing — driving a wave of graduate student organizing. These elections tend to have very high win rates, a recent study from the Economic Policy Institute finds. Workers at Starbucks, Amazon, and Trader Joe's — all part of the recent voting wave — haven't made it there yet. https://www.axios.com/2024/06/20/union-elections-nlrb-win-rate
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.dc:creator: doxydad1

item.title: It's my cake day, I decided to rant.
item.description: I'm 71 and just got 11 years on Imgur and 28 years continued sobriety. I was looking at all the posts by people who are stressed out and looked back at my own past. I grew up during the atomic scare. They taught us to hide under our desks (presumably so they could sweep up our dust easier later) when the sirens blared. They tested the sirens once a week and occasionally at a random time. We were told to be afraid of quicksand. The Bermuda Triangle was going to swallow us. The commies under our beds would gut us and rape our women. Space Aliens! Area 51! The Cold war. When I see the shit people are fed now I wonder what the future will look like. PTSD caused by media BS is killing us. There are REAL shootings to traumatize us. Kids have to learn what to do in case of shooters. The KKK and the Nazis are making a resurgence. Try to watch "entertainment" for long and it will be murders, hatred and more murders. The news? No. Then I realized it's how the elite keep control. They even profit by spreading fear. My only thought is BRAVE UP and KILL THE RICH.
item.dc:creator: donsterkay

item.title: Images of Note: Gay men Yaoi
item.description:
Rainbow Rat! Rainbow Rat!
Hells yeah!
Neil, thank you for this cursed content.
post what knuckles is looking at
The reason why they don't want words added to the dictionary is to deny people the words to describe themselves.
I know it's over four years old, but I love how people reacted to the Tarantulas by trying to catch them when in Lockdown.
Straight women Yaoi: Charming Prettyboys Gay men Yaoi: Himbo Bear Lesbian Yaoi: Sleep deprived Gobbo. Straight men Yaoi: His name was Robert Paulson.
Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee Coffee
Your Petrol Stations have Aisles?
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream (m/w/f around 1830 UK time) http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging

item.title: Vancouver Retro Gaming Expo 2024
item.description:
Cosplay of Dale Gribble from King of the Hill, test photo before heading to convention
The Wavebirds gaming-themed ska band
GRIDCASE personal laptops
GRID laptops being used for sentry targeting systems
NABU PC playing Pac-Man, plus cool old Sony screen with a handle
Snatcher (I think) on PC-8801
Castlevania Chronicles on Sharp X68000, featuring Hudson Soft Joy Card
1970 "Four-Phase Systems" model IV-70, one of the earliest semiconductor memory computers
PC-98 playing Puyo Puyo
Simpsons S05E15 "Deep Space Homer" being played on a TRS-80
Simpsons S05E15 "Deep Space Homer" being played on a TRS-80
Tandy 1100 FD laptop
Tandy Color Computer 2 with attached printer, tape drive, cartridge reader, and floppy disk drive
Various ZX Spectrum models, playing Monty on the Run
Crowd shot of New Westminster Anvil Centre
The Koopa Troopas video game cover band
Photo of cosplayers gathering before cosplay contest, and gaming free play room
Cosplayer with Lagiacrus Armor from Monster Hunter
Street Fighter cosplayers and panel of judges
VRGE2024 Cosplay Contest
VIC-20 playing Donkey Kong (Officially licensed in the 80s) and Commodore 64 playing Galencia (more recent homebrew space shooter game)
Cosplay of Ravio from The Legend of Zelda: A Link Between Worlds
Dick Gumshoe from Phoenix Wright
Game Boy Camera / Game Boy Printer photo of my Dale Gribble cosplay

item.title: Tim Burton's "BATMAN" was released 35 years ago today in 1989
item.description:
The original trailer was so last-minute, it was edited together before they had Danny Elfman's iconic soundtrack to go with it - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y7Bc69q9hXk
The late Dave Lea, Michael Keaton's stunt double for this film as well as "Batman Returns". He passed away two years ago, he also helped to train and choreograph fight sequences for scores of other actors/actresses. He studying multiple forms of martial arts under some of the most prominent instructors in the field, including Dan Inosanto (who trained under Bruce Lee). Among the many styles he studied, Lea trained in Shotokan Karate, Aikido, Escrima, Wing Chun Kung Fu, Shaolin Kung Fu, Fu Jow Pai, White Crane Kung Fu, Leopard Kung Fu, Snake Kung Fu, Southern Dragon Kung Fu, Hapkune-Do and Kickboxing.
Ghostbusters - https://imgur.com/gallery/LsBvyll Doctor Who - https://imgur.com/gallery/JxXJqzd Stargate - https://imgur.com/gallery/nXYrraP TMNT - https://imgur.com/gallery/WzuWWxX Star Trek TOS - https://imgur.com/gallery/4SPJxyX Star Trek 3 - https://imgur.com/gallery/fTMuvU6 TNG - https://imgur.com/gallery/CMgsCIy DS9 - https://imgur.com/gallery/S8ISUlZ VOY - https://imgur.com/gallery/YDc2PD2 ENT - https://imgur.com/gallery/ov74iIE 09 - https://imgur.com/gallery/Odj24Cz Star Wars Ep1 - https://imgur.com/gallery/gkRgfe9 The Goonies - https://imgur.com/gallery/Ek3MpLl The Muppet Movie - https://imgur.com/a/ZA96OBI Xena - https://imgur.com/gallery/Hws7HQl The Mummy - https://imgur.com/gallery/GUrb6Oa Speed - https://imgur.com/gallery/TQ9uQuP Mary Poppins - https://imgur.com/gallery/f6WgNlR The Lion King - https://imgur.com/gallery/nAtmrFU Sesame Street - https://imgur.com/gallery/f3RafRD Dragonslayer - https://imgur.com/a/YSwNblp The Great Race - https://imgur.com/gallery/tO9DeWn Black Lagoon - https://imgur.com/gallery/EraNuoW Chip 'n Dale - https://imgur.com/gallery/L6uxizO
The gigantic miniature-scale set used to depict Gotham City, 1989.
Turns out the boots used in the Batsuit were just modified Air Jordans
From the original "pilot", made in the summer of 1991. THE DARK KNIGHT'S FIRST NIGHT. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hMucplM3CUk It was used to pitch the series (utilizing Danny Elfman's score since they hadn't yet gotten Shirley Walker to compose any original music for the series) to the execs at Fox Kids. Animated quickly in Canada by friends, the show itself would eventually be made by master animation studios in Japan and South Korea.

item.title: Voting Third Party Is Voting For Trump
item.description: https://x.com/marceelias/status/1804922106897219867 He even says so himself. It’s why Bannon helped push RFK into the election. And every time I mention this absolute fact you get third party wankers rushing out to argue against it with their excuses. Same for those who refuse to vote too. Most of them are usually Trump supporters anyway, ones just about smart enough to know they can’t publicly admit that without losing all credibility, so they start playing these ‘both sides’ games.
